title: HTML Fixtures, Fake Transports, Local Servers, Properties, Faults, and Mutation · Program Brief: Fault Injection
summary: Tests hit live websites, fixtures contain one perfect page, retries never fail midway, URL properties are untested, and snapshots pass after a broken selector returns nothing. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the fault injection increment in layered deterministic crawler verification suite. Learners progress through prediction…
objectives: Inject DNS, connect, TLS, redirect, truncated body, corrupt encoding, parser, queue, disk, cancellation, browser, and checkpoint failures.
Test URL resolution, idempotent canonical keys, scope monotonicity, fragment behavior, query policies, and adversarial address forms as properties.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-fault-injection — Inject DNS, connect, TLS, redirect, truncated body, corrupt encoding, parser, queue, disk, cancellation, browser, and checkpoint failures.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-testing-fixtures-faults-4
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-testing-fixtures-faults-4.
# Case 1655: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def worked_scraper_testing_fixtures_faults_4(fixtures: int = 72, changed_cases: int = 36, injected_faults: int = 18, killed_mutants: int = 16, live_public_requests: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorizationworked4",
        "url1655",
        "response4dekrow",
        "treeworked41655",
        "frontier1655worked4",
        "report4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if fixtures < 24 or changed_cases < 12:
        return False, fixtures, "test-depth"
    if injected_faults < 8:
        return False, injected_faults, "fault-depth"
    if killed_mutants < 8:
        return False, killed_mutants, "mutation-evidence"
    if live_public_requests > 0:
        return False, live_public_requests, "nondeterministic-live-test"
    assert killed_mutants >= 8 and live_public_requests == 0
    return True, killed_mutants, "layered-crawler-verification"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Fault Injection model · Theory Fault Injection
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Fault Injection: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 9373 during a privacy-minimized internal crawl. Build deterministic Python evidence to combine fixed HTML, fake transports, local servers, URL properties, fault injection, and mutation evidence; personal data must remain minimized and redacted; then inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject DNS, connect, TLS, redirect, truncated body, corrupt encoding, parser, queue, disk, cancellation, browser, and checkpoint failures.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inject DNS, connect, TLS, redirect, truncated body, corrupt encoding, parser, queue, disk, cancellation, browser, and checkpoint failures.
